description: True/false evaluation for the a4g-ui-test skill. Anchored on the structured text artifact the skill emits when run: its resolved-config / delegation summary and the downstream PASS/FAIL report, NOT on screenshots or live app state.

evals.statement: The emitted summary echoes the --problem value as the thing being fixed or verified.
If no --max-iter is supplied, the reported max developer-reviewer iterations is exactly 2 (the documented default), not blank or some other number.
The summary does NOT report --keep-evidence as enabled (true) when the invocation omitted the flag, since its documented default is false.
When an --issue number is provided, the report names that exact issue number as the one to close on PASS; when omitted, the report references no issue-close action.
Every exercise/delegation path reported in the output is an absolute path, not a relative one.
The reported EXERCISE_PATH resolves under <PROJECT_DIR>/.claude/skills/a4g-ui-test/exercises/ and ends in a Python file from that exercises directory.
The output reports ensuring a .ui-review-evidence entry exists in .gitignore (added if missing, left alone if present).
The gitignore step does NOT append a duplicate .ui-review-evidence line when one is already present (the grep guard suppresses re-adding it).
The delegation summary reports the start command as exactly 'bash scripts/live-test.sh'.
The reported --ready-url is exactly http://localhost:8765/api/commands/mode (the API readiness probe), not the frontend URL.
The reported dashboard --url is exactly http://localhost:5173.
The reported --pages list contains exactly the six dashboard routes (root, /#/stats, /#/build-orders, /#/replays, /#/decisions, /#/training) with no missing or extra page.
The reported viewport is exactly 1920x1080.
The reported stop-signal is exactly 'process-group', not a per-process kill or other value.

Optional flags (--issue, --max-iter, --keep-evidence, --record-video, --record-har) appear in the delegation invocation ONLY when the user supplied them; absent flags are not passed with fabricated values.
The problem statement passed to reviewers includes an ALPHA4GATE CONTEXT block, not the bare user problem alone.
The domain context lists all six dashboard tabs by name: Live, Stats, Build Orders, Replays, Decisions, Training.
The domain context states the API server is at localhost:8765 and the dashboard at localhost:5173 (ports not swapped).
The domain context preserves the literal backend log patterns 'Cmd OK: <action> <target>' (executed) and 'Cmd FAIL: <action> <target>' (failed) without altering their semantics.
The domain context states the SC2 game is started manually by the user and the skill starts only the bot + API + frontend.
When no --exercise-cmd is given, the chosen exercise is test_commands.py (the documented default), not test_mode_switch.py or test_mute.py.
The output surfaces the SC2-must-be-running prerequisite (the skill cannot start SC2 itself) before or alongside launching the flow.
The output is valid, parseable text with each delegation arg appearing at most once (no contradictory duplicate --url / --ready-url / --pages values).

The final PASS/FAIL report grounds its verdict in cited evidence: every per-page or per-flow verdict names the specific screenshot or evidence artifact (filename/path under the evidence directory) it rests on, rather than asserting a result with no cited capture.
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 16, 30, 33-34 (durable output is a verdict; review-proof / evidence-citation discipline per dev/.claude/rules — no claim without observed evidence)

evals.statement: The report explicitly states a per-page verdict for ALL SIX target pages (Live/root, Stats, Build Orders, Replays, Decisions, Training); none of the six is silently omitted or left unmentioned.
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 63-64, 81 (six pages enumerated in --pages and domain context; coverage completeness)

evals.statement: The report does NOT issue an overall PASS verdict over any page or flow that the same output describes as broken, errored, blank, or failing (no false-positive PASS contradicting a described failure).
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 16, 88 (visual + log evidence; failure log patterns) — false-positive discipline

evals.statement: If the run, exercise, or any page capture failed (startup error, Playwright timeout, missing screenshot), the report says so explicitly and does NOT fabricate a PASS/FAIL verdict for the un-captured page; it reports the capture failure instead.
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 116-119 (prerequisites can fail), 33-34 — false-negative / no-fabrication discipline

evals.statement: Every visual or behavioral claim in the report (e.g. 'command appears in history with executed badge', 'Live page shows minerals/gas/supply') is tied to a cited capture or log line; the report makes no visual claim that is not backed by a named piece of observed evidence.
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 84-90 (the visual/behavioral facts reviewers assert) — grounding: no visual claim without cited capture

evals.statement: The report distinguishes the command-flow / exercise outcome (did the submitted command reach the history and get an executed/rejected status badge) as its own cited verdict, separate from the static per-page render verdicts, rather than collapsing the dynamic exercise result into a single undifferentiated overall PASS.
evals.source: SKILL.md lines 14-16, 97-101, 84-86 (the command submit -> WS broadcast -> history flow is the primary thing under test)
